user_query:
I'm organizing a small reading session on Sino–Japanese normalization and need your help to compile materials and draft outreach emails.

Using the input files input/recipients.csv and input/brief.md, please do the following:

1) Web research and structured catalog
- Use a search engine to find at least 5 publicly accessible, official digitized primary documents related to PRC–Japan relations from 1968–1978 (e.g., the 1972 normalization/Joint Communiqué, foreign ministry statements, legislative or parliamentary records, national archives items, or university digital collections). Target sources should come from official institutions such as government ministries, national libraries/archives, or universities.
- For each selected item, record the following fields and save them to a CSV at workspace/out/sources.csv:
  title, issuing_body, year (original document year if available), language (e.g., zh/ja/en), document_type (e.g., communiqué, speech, archival record), source_domain (e.g., mofa.go.jp, ndl.go.jp, archives.gov.cn, etc.), url, access_date (YYYY-MM-DD), notes (1–2 lines on relevance to normalization or the 1968–1978 period).
- Include items from at least two different institutions (as reflected in source_domain and issuing_body). Aim to include items in Chinese or Japanese when possible; if unsure, label language as best as you can.
- Also write a simple search log to workspace/out/search_log.jsonl where each line is one JSON object with at least: {"query": string, "engine": string, "rationale": string, "timestamp": ISO 8601}. Log all distinct queries you used to locate the selected documents.

2) Draft two personalized emails
- Read input/recipients.csv for recipient details and input/brief.md for the seminar context (title/date/description). Draft two separate email messages tailored to each recipient and save them as:
  - workspace/out/emails/email_to_li_wei.txt
  - workspace/out/emails/email_to_sato_hiroshi.txt
- Each email must:
  - Begin with a Subject line (e.g., "Subject: [short topic]") followed by a greeting using the recipient’s name.
  - Briefly introduce the seminar using details from input/brief.md (title and date) and explain why you’re reaching out.
  - Recommend 2–3 items drawn from workspace/out/sources.csv. For each recommended item, include the exact title and issuing_body as listed in the CSV. Prefer items whose "language" matches the recipient’s language_preference from recipients.csv when possible; if not possible, still recommend the most relevant items.
  - Close with a polite request for feedback or additional suggestions.

Deliverables to create under the workspace directory:
- workspace/out/sources.csv (as specified)
- workspace/out/search_log.jsonl (one JSON object per line with query details)
- workspace/out/emails/email_to_li_wei.txt
- workspace/out/emails/email_to_sato_hiroshi.txt

Please do not send emails—just produce the drafts and files above. Do not hardcode any direct URLs in this request; find the documents via your own search queries and record the URLs you choose in the CSV. Ensure that the emails reference titles that exactly match entries in workspace/out/sources.csv. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
